events.label: assistant
events.text: Diagnosis written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: the impulse lines are installed backwards for their service — gas taps should be on top with continuous upward slope (so condensate drains back to process), liquid taps should be on bottom/side with continuous downward slope (so entrained gas vents back). As installed, the gas leg will trap condensate (false head/offset) and the liquid leg will trap vapor (gas-locking, erratic signal). Since the transmitter is already in service, this is a `fail` with `high` risk requiring isolation, re-routing/re-sloping per API RP 551, leak test, and recalibration before return to service.
